generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance AngularPositionEncoderLeft : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision position encoder measuring angular position of the MABI MAX 100's left axis for real-time motion monitoring in machining operations. Integrated with Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl control system for positional feedback."
    DTDFVocab:producedData AngularPositionDataLeft
]

instance AngularPositionEncoderRight : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision position encoder measuring angular position of the MABI MAX 100's right axis for real-time motion monitoring in machining operations. Integrated with Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl control system for positional feedback."
    DTDFVocab:producedData AngularPositionDataRight
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance AngularPositionDataLeft : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AngularPositionEncoderLeft
]

instance AngularPositionDataRight : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AngularPositionEncoderRight
]

instance TorqueVelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AngularPositionEncoderLeft
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AngularPositionEncoderRight
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance FrictionIdentificationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based white-box model using Simulink for torque-velocity dynamics analysis and friction coefficient identification in robotic machining processes. Leverages contextual data traces for purpose-specific abstraction."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkProcessingEnabler
]

instance ContextualDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated sensor data trace combining torque, velocity, and position metrics from the physical robot system for friction analysis purposes. Serves as input for both modeling and visualization services."
    DTDFVocab:fromData AngularPositionDataLeft
    DTDFVocab:fromData AngularPositionDataRight
    DTDFVocab:fromData TorqueVelocityData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationPipeline
]

instance OperationalLogTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed operational log data containing derived friction coefficients and machining parameters for decision support in tool life optimization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ContextualDataTrace
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DecisionSupportEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MQTTDataTransport : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation (Mosquitto server/client) for streaming time-series sensor data from physical robot to digital twin with continuous publish-subscribe model. Enables one-way data flow for passive digital shadow functionality."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance SimulinkProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink environment for detailed behavioral modeling of torque-velocity dynamics and friction identification using physics-based simulation models."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance DataAggregationPipeline : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom C++/Java processing pipeline for aggregating raw sensor data into contextual data traces and operational log metrics with traceability support."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService
    DTDFVocab:enables DecisionSupportService
]

instance VisualizationDashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-compatible visualization interface enabling dashboard-based monitoring of friction-related parameters and operational anomalies for manufacturing engineers."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance DecisionSupportEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-based decision support framework leveraging transitive traceability of data traces to validate recommendations against reference models and simulation outputs."
    DTDFVocab:enables DecisionSupportService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service collecting and processing contextual sensor data traces from physical robot into higher-level abstractions for friction analysis and operational monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionAnalysisInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalStatusInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing real-time dashboard visualization of friction coefficients and machining parameters for human operators and engineers."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service performing physics-based friction identification using Simulink models and contextual data traces for process optimization in machining operations."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionOptimizationInsight
]

instance DecisionSupportService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service generating purpose-specific insights for manufacturing engineers based on validated operational data traces and friction analysis results."
    DTDFVocab:provides ToolLifeRecommendationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing real-time friction coefficient analysis derived from processed torque-velocity data traces for machining process optimization."
]

instance OperationalStatusInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight monitoring current operational status of the robotic machining system based on aggregated sensor data and contextual parameters."
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight enabling interactive visualization of friction-related parameters through dashboard interfaces for engineering analysis."
]

instance FrictionOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight offering recommendations for friction reduction strategies based on validated simulation results and operational data traces."
]

instance ToolLifeRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing tool life optimization recommendations derived from friction analysis and operational log data for manufacturing planning."
]

// C1: System under study
instance MachiningRobotDigitalShadow : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Passive digital shadow of MABI MAX 100 machining robot focused on friction identification and modeling in advanced robotics manufacturing applications."
    base:contains PhysicalRobotSystem
    base:contains DigitalModelComponents
]

instance PhysicalRobotSystem : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical industrial robot operating under dynamic load conditions in machining processes with integrated position encoders for real-time data collection."
]

instance DigitalModelComponents : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Digital representation including physics-based models, data processing components, and visualization interfaces for friction analysis in machining operations."
    base:contains TorqueVelocitySensorAssembly
    base:contains FrictionAnalysisModelAssembly
    base:contains VisualizationInterface
]

// C2: Components
instance RobotFirstAxis : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "First axis component of MABI MAX 100 robot containing interconnected mechanical elements (motors, gearboxes) modeled using SysML Internal Block Diagrams for structural representation."
    base:isContainedIn PhysicalRobotSystem
]

instance TorqueVelocitySensorAssembly : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Sensor assembly combining angular position encoders and torque measurement devices for capturing dynamic machining parameters in real-time."
    base:isContainedIn PhysicalRobotSystem
]

instance FrictionAnalysisModelAssembly : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Component assembly integrating physics-based friction model with data processing pipeline for contextual analysis of machining operations."
    base:isContainedIn DigitalModelComponents
]

instance VisualizationInterface : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "SysML-based visualization component providing interactive dashboards for friction parameter monitoring and analysis in manufacturing environments."
    base:isContainedIn DigitalModelComponents
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in the described Digital Twin system is **not explicitly detailed** in the provided documents. The text distinguishes **digital shadows** (passive, one-way data flows) from **Digital Twins**, which may include **active components** ([32], [35]–[37]) but does not specify **control flows**, **command types**, or **feedback mechanisms**. Key gaps include:
- **Command formats** (e.g., REST APIs, OPC UA, or proprietary protocols).
- **Validation/authentication** (e.g., JWT, TLS, or role-based access control).
- **Conflict resolution** (e.g., priority-based or consensus algorithms).
- **Feedback systems** (e.g., acknowledgment messages or error handling).
- **Execution confirmation** (e.g., timestamps, checksums, or status codes).
Since the documents focus on **digital shadows** and **modeling methodologies** ([56], [59]) rather than bidirectional control, this characteristic is **Not in Document**."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system described in the documents emphasizes **temporal synchronization** between physical and digital components through structured **data trace aggregation** and **processing pipelines**, where data traces are collected at variable granularity depending on the use case (e.g., manufacturing injection molding). While explicit synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz, ms) are not detailed, the system relies on **real-time or near-real-time processing** of data traces to maintain consistency between the physical system and its digital representation. Latency requirements are implicitly addressed through the use of **processing components** (e.g., simulation or data calculation models) that compute higher-level abstractions from input data traces, ensuring traceability and temporal coherence. The **reference model** (e.g., UML class diagrams) supports defining conformance relations for time-sensitive operations, though no specific time-scale metrics (e.g., microsecond-level precision) are provided in the documents. The system’s temporal behavior is governed by the **purpose-driven design** of digital shadows/twins, where data traces are aggregated and abstracted to meet specific objectives, such as predictive maintenance or process optimization."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s **orchestration and coordination** are framed within a **reference model-based approach**, where the **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (UML class diagrams) serves as a foundational structure for defining conforming models. This model incorporates **processing components** (e.g., simulation or data calculation models) that consume input data traces and produce output data, enabling **fine-grained traceability** of operations. The system’s architecture leverages **architecture description languages (ADLs)** (e.g., SysML IBDs, MontiArc) to describe components, connectors, and composition, while **model-based systems engineering (MBSE)** methodologies (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM) ensure hierarchical abstraction. Coordination is achieved through **conforming models** that incarnate reference concepts (e.g., classes, associations) into concrete elements, allowing for **customizable orchestration** tailored to use cases like injection molding. Resource management is implied via the **processing logic** embedded in simulation or calculation models, which abstracts and aggregates data traces. The system does not explicitly detail high-level orchestration patterns (e.g., microservices, event-driven architectures) but emphasizes **traceability-driven coordination** via transitive associations between data traces, processing components, and models."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system, as inferred from the provided documents, is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, which decompose system modeling into hierarchical abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. The lifecycle transitions between these stages are governed by **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)**, **ACME**, **AADL**, or **MontiArc**, which define components, interfaces, connectors, and composition rules. Behavior modeling is further refined using **SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)**, **state machines (SysML v1.7)**, or **MontiArc Automaton**, often integrated with simulation tools like **MathWorks Simulink**, **general-purpose languages (C++, Java)**, or other computational frameworks. The lifecycle also incorporates **data traceability**—where data traces originate from processing components (e.g., simulation or calculation models) and are aggregated via **aggregation and abstraction mechanisms** for contextual purposes. Metadata supports further processing, ensuring traceability of data traces to their generating components and models. The lifecycle governance is implicitly tied to **conformance relations** in reference models (e.g., UML Class Diagrams), where concrete realizations (concretizations) of reference models are developed to align with specific use cases, such as manufacturing or cyber-physical systems (CPS)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system emphasizes **fidelity and validity** through **continuous, multi-physics-aware identification** of models, ensuring alignment between simulated and measured data. Validation is achieved by comparing **deviation of simulated data versus measured data**, with a focus on **traceability** to maintain data provenance. The system employs **model-based development methodologies** to verify correctness of transformations (e.g., from SysML to simulation models), ensuring consistency across heterogeneous tools. Uncertainty quantification is implied through the integration of **data-driven and physics-based models**, where black-box approaches (e.g., machine learning) mitigate gaps in white-box models. However, specific metrics (e.g., error thresholds, statistical methods) or quality assurance processes (e.g., Statistical Process Control) are not explicitly mentioned in the documents.
"
]

    base:desc "
The **technical connection** for the digital shadow system is limited to the **MQTT protocol** ([52]) for streaming sensor data from physical objects to the digital representation. The documents do not mention security protocols, reliability assurances, or specific communication infrastructure beyond the protocol itself. The system relies on MQTT for data transmission but does not describe additional layers such as IoT gateways, VPNs, or encryption standards.

Supported by: '[streamed via MQTT]' (implied by [52])."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with external systems through **standardized protocols and interoperability frameworks**, primarily leveraging **MQTT for streaming sensor data** and **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)** for asset-centric data modeling. Integration is facilitated by **heterogeneous stakeholders and perspectives**, requiring the system to accommodate diverse modeling notations (e.g., SysML, ADLs) and tools. While the documents emphasize **data-driven integration** (e.g., streaming time series data), they do not explicitly detail **plug-and-play mechanisms** or broader enterprise systems (e.g., ERP). The focus remains on **cyber-physical system (CPS) components** (e.g., robots, controllers) and their digital representations, with integration constrained to the **purpose-specific Digital Shadow Reference Model** and its conforming implementations. Dependencies on external systems are implied for data provision (e.g., sensors) but lack specification of broader architectural patterns."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system incorporates **physical safety constraints** for robotic operations, where the **safety cell** of the robotic system (e.g., MABI robotic arm) restricts the maximum working range to **[-90°, 90°]**, ensuring collision avoidance and operator safety. Sensor data, including time-series telemetry, is transmitted via **MQTT protocol** (as referenced in [52]), which implies a need for **secure communication channels** to prevent unauthorized access or data tampering. However, the provided documents do not specify **authentication mechanisms (e.g., OAuth 2.0)**, **encryption standards (e.g., AES-256)**, **safety integrity levels (SIL)**, or **functional safety compliance (e.g., ISO 13849)**. No explicit **risk assessment frameworks** (e.g., FMEA, HAZOP) or **fail-safe mechanisms** (e.g., emergency stop protocols) are documented. The system’s safety is implicitly tied to the **physical boundaries of the robotic workspace** and **sensor data integrity**, but no formal cybersecurity or safety certification standards are mentioned."
]
